title: T-096 — releaseNpdProjectToFactory canonical NPD Builder release orchestrator

pipeline_inputs.description: Canonical releaseNpdProjectToFactory action covering WIP/FG/shared BOM/factory_spec/status transaction and blockers.
pipeline_inputs.details: NPD Builder release transaction invokes G3 FG mapping, shared BOM SSOT writer, Technical factory_spec API, RM usability validation and release status model. D365 optional only.
scope_files: Existing NPD Builder/release action paths discovered before editing
out_of_scope: Technical approval UI (cross-module, may be 03-TECH task)
acceptance_criteria: Given DEV-123 has linked brief, mapped FG, all required departments closed and G4 approval, when releaseNpdProjectToFactory runs, then WIP/intermediate items + FG mapping + initial shared BOM + factory_spec draft/in_review + release audit/events are created in one idempotent transaction.
Given any BOM component has RM usability FAIL, when release is requested, then it aborts with typed blockers and creates no partial factory-usable state.
Given release is retried for the same project/version, then no duplicate WIP/FG/BOM/spec versions are created.
Given D365 integration is disabled, when release runs, then Monopilot release still succeeds and no D365 export is attempted.
Given Technical approval is missing, when release completes, then status is pending_technical_approval and factory/Planning consumers cannot use the FG.
test_strategy: RED unit tests for preflight blockers and idempotency.
RED integration test for transaction rollback on RM usability failure.
RED event-ordering test for release_requested → released_records_created → pending_technical_approval.
risk_red_lines: Do not perform D365 export inside release transaction.
Do not mark FG/BOM/spec factory-usable before Technical approval.
Do not bypass shared BOM SSOT or create NPD-only BOM tables.
Do not ignore RM usability blockers.
